title: Delicate Crispy Chinese Pork Mu Shu
description: A pungent and savory Chinese dish featuring crispy pork, vegetables, and a cheese-pull effect, perfect for a brunch mood. This high-protein friendly recipe uses warm water and air-fry methods for a healthier twist.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, hoisin sauce, and sesame oil to make the marinade.
Add the sliced pork to the marinade and let it sit for 10 minutes.
Heat the air fryer to 400 degrees Fahrenheit and cook the marinated pork for 10 minutes, or until crispy.
In a separate pan, heat 1 tablespoon of sesame oil and saute the sliced mushrooms until they are tender.
Add the shredded cabbage and chopped scallions to the pan and cook until the cabbage is wilted.
In a small bowl, mix together the cornstarch and warm water to make a slurry.
Add the slurry to the pan with the vegetables and cook for 1 minute, or until the sauce has thickened.
To assemble the mu shu, place a portion of the cooked vegetables onto a tortilla, followed by a few slices of the crispy pork and a sprinkle of shredded mozzarella cheese.
Fold the bottom half of the tortilla up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the mu shu into a tight cylinder.
Serve the mu shu hot and enjoy the cheese-pull effect.
